editors_note.headline: This week
editors_note.thesis: This week's signal is clear: business model decisions are moving faster than product roadmaps. If you're not tracking Anthropic vs. the Pentagon: What’s actually at stake, you're already behind.
editors_note.hook: The headline drives the week, but the real question is what it means for your vendor assumptions and Q2 plans.
editors_note.spotlight: The lead story is the most likely to change near-term decisions for teams shipping AI products.
editors_note.underhyped: Mixture of Experts (MoEs) in Transformers deserves more attention than it's getting.
editors_note.risk: Keep an eye on Joint Statement from OpenAI and Microsoft — it could shift budgets or vendor assumptions.

lead_story.title: Anthropic vs. the Pentagon: What’s actually at stake?
lead_story.what_happened: Anthropic in the news; could affect pricing, roadmaps, or compliance this quarter — flag it.
lead_story.our_take: The signal is real; the timing matters more than the headline. Use it as a planning input (TechCrunch AI).
lead_story.what_to_do: Bring Anthropic to your next planning sync — it may affect Q2 priorities.

articles.title: MySQL 9.6 Changes Foreign Key Constraints and Cascade Handling
articles.summary: Depending on your setup, MySQL might save you time or headaches down the line (via InfoQ).
articles.what_happened: Quick read on MySQL — may or may not matter depending on your stack.
articles.why_it_matters: Keep an eye on MySQL — small moves here tend to become bigger ones.
articles.what_to_do: Review MySQL and decide on one concrete follow-up action by end of week.
articles.our_take: Don't let FOMO drive architecture. Validate on your own terms before changing course.

sections.title: What changed in industry
sections.deck: Company moves, launches, and policy shifts that could affect your roadmap.
sections.promise: What changed and why it matters.

articles.title: Joint Statement from OpenAI and Microsoft
articles.summary: The OpenAI story has enough signal to track — file it for your next planning round (via OpenAI Blog).
articles.what_happened: OpenAI could ripple into pricing or vendor talks — add it to your next planning sync.
articles.why_it_matters: Industry moves around OpenAI tend to show up in vendor and budget conversations; get ahead of it.
articles.what_to_do: Add OpenAI as a discussion item in your next strategy or planning meeting.
articles.our_take: The signal is real; the timing matters more than the headline. Use it as a planning input.

articles.title: Trump orders federal agencies to drop Anthropic’s AI
articles.summary: Depending on your setup, Trump might save you time or headaches down the line (via The Verge AI).
articles.what_happened: A move around Trump is worth a slot in your next ops or leadership review.
articles.why_it_matters: The Trump move could shift the math for your next planning cycle; flag it in leadership review.
articles.what_to_do: Flag Trump for your leadership team — it may shift near-term priorities.
articles.our_take: The signal is real; the timing matters more than the headline. Use it as a planning input (The Verge AI).

sections.title: Tools to try
sections.deck: Tools worth testing in a real workflow this week.

articles.title: Mixture of Experts (MoEs) in Transformers
articles.summary: The Mixture story has enough signal to track — file it for your next planning round (via Hugging Face Blog).
articles.what_happened: If Mixture delivers on its claims, it could simplify a few steps in your current setup.
articles.why_it_matters: Mixture is worth evaluating if your current tools are hitting limits.
articles.what_to_do: Spin up Mixture in a 2-hour proof of concept — compare output against your current tool.
articles.our_take: Ship a proof of concept before forming an opinion. The API surface matters more than the feature list.

articles.title: How we built Agent Builder’s memory system
articles.summary: If Agent Builder pans out, it could change how teams approach their current stack (via LangChain Blog).
articles.what_happened: Agent Builder is tackling a pain point that most teams work around rather than solve.
articles.why_it_matters: Adopting Agent Builder could speed delivery if integration friction stays low.
articles.what_to_do: Allocate one afternoon to test Agent Builder against your top use case.
articles.our_take: Tools are only as good as the problem they solve for you. Try it on a real task, not a tutorial.

sections.title: Open-source picks
sections.deck: Open-source projects worth a closer look.

articles.title: llmfit
articles.summary: The llmfit story has enough signal to track — file it for your next planning round (via GitHub Trending (AI/ML)).
articles.what_happened: The llmfit repo is active and well-maintained — worth a test drive.
articles.why_it_matters: Llmfit traction suggests builder demand, but longevity depends on maintainer commitment.
articles.what_to_do: Star llmfit for later, but check maintainer activity and issue backlog before depending on it.
articles.our_take: A trending repo is a signal, not a recommendation. Do your diligence before depending on it.

articles.title: openfang
articles.summary: Open-source Agent Operating System. Currently at 4,646 stars with ~549 stars/day growth.
articles.why_it_matters: Openfang being open gives teams use to negotiate or self-host.
articles.what_to_do: Check openfang for recent activity, open issues, and license compatibility this week.
articles.our_take: Popularity and production-readiness are different things. Check the last commit date and open issues first.

prompt_of_the_week.text: Rewrite this API documentation to be developer-friendly. Keep it accurate but make it scannable with clear examples.
prompt_of_the_week.why: Bad docs are the #1 reason developers abandon tools. Asking for 'scannable with clear examples' targets the two things devs actually need: quick answers and copy-pasteable code.
prompt_of_the_week.tip: Paste the existing docs. Specify your audience (junior devs, senior engineers, etc.).
